behavior_summary: Provides a minimal 'U8P_BUG::look' (inspect) handler that displays a single bark string "Mandril " when the player examines this NPC/item.

Implements a hit handler U8P_BUG::gotHit(uword, word) that gates hit propagation with several guards: ignores some large 'this' values, skips processing if the NPC is dead, and—when conditions pass—plays a hit SFX and forwards the hit to Item::receiveHit with fixed constants and a runtime value.
The hit-handler uses item-related queries (weight or id check) to decide whether to propagate the hit, and plays SFX id 0x55 when propagation occurs.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BUG::look: trivial control flow — emits a single bark then suspends/returns.
U8P_BUG::gotHit: early-exit guard comparing a runtime 'this' value against 0x0100 (skip if above threshold).
U8P_BUG::gotHit: calls Npc::isDead(addressof(local2)) and aborts if the NPC is dead.
U8P_BUG::gotHit: requires 'this' nonzero and either Item::getWeightIncludingContents(local1) >= 3 or local1 == 1 to play SFX and call Item::receiveHit.
open_questions: What exactly does the second 'word' parameter represent at runtime (damage amount, flags, or a special 'this' token)?
What are the precise meanings of the numeric constants passed to Item::receiveHit (0x04, 0x0096, 0x08, 0x01) in game terms?
Is the comparison against 0x0100 an object-type sentinel, a priority/bitmask guard, or a damage threshold?
What does item id == 1 represent in the game's item taxonomy (special-case lightweight/heavy item)?

main_function.name: U8P_BUG::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to player 'U8P_BUG::look/examine' by presenting a short descriptive bark string for the NPC/item.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player inspects/examines this NPC/item (standard U8P_BUG::look/inspect action, processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_BUG::look
function_map.role: inspect/U8P_BUG::look handler that displays the short description/bark

function_map.name: U8P_BUG::gotHit
function_map.role: hit/damage handler that gates and forwards hits to item damage logic

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the referenced NPC/object is dead and abort hit processing if so.
Query an item's weight (including contents) to decide whether to propagate the hit (weight >= 3 inferred).
Play a hit sound effect (sound id 0x55) when the hit propagation conditions are satisfied.
Forward/apply the hit to the item/entity using several constant parameters and the runtime 'this' value.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides a minimal 'U8P_BUG::look' (inspect) handler that displays a single bark string "Mandril " when the player examines this NPC/item.
